data.title: OMER S.p.A.: Resilient Fundamentals and Structural Growth Support Buy Rating Despite Softer FY26 Outlook
data.description:
Intermonte analyst Gianluca Bertuzzo FCA maintained a Buy rating on OMER S.p.a. yesterday and set a price target of €4.70.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Gianluca Bertuzzo FCA has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that underline OMER S.p.A.’s resilience and structural strengths despite a softer near-term outlook. The company closed FY25 with better-than-expected results, driven by the DolceVita project and solid core operations, while maintaining a robust financial profile with significant net cash and healthy free cash flow.

Gianluca Bertuzzo FCA’s rating is also based on OMER’s sizeable backlog, strong positioning with leading train manufacturers, and exposure to a sector characterized by high entry barriers and long‑term structural growth. Although FY26 is expected to see lower revenues and margins due to project phase-outs and inflation, visibility on future orders, supportive industry dynamics, and attractive returns on invested capital justify a target price above the current share price and a continued Buy recommendation.

9WR’s price has also changed moderately for the past six months – from EUR4.550 to EUR3.400, which is a -25.27% drop .

data.title: Compelling Growth and Undervalued Opportunity in T Stamp’s Identity Authentication Platform
data.description:
Analyst Allen Klee of Maxim Group reiterated a Buy rating on T Stamp, Inc. Class A, with a price target of $12.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Allen Klee has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including T Stamp’s specialized role in identity authentication and its growing commercial traction. He highlights that the firm’s technology converts biometric and other sensitive information into secure tokenized identifiers, positioning it well as organizations seek stronger data protection and privacy-compliant solutions.

Klee also points to a strong multi-year growth outlook supported by international expansion, new product offerings, and acquisitions that broaden the platform, with his model calling for rapid revenue acceleration in 2026 and 2027. In addition, he views the current valuation as compelling, noting that IDAI trades at a steep discount to peers on an EV-to-revenue basis, while his $12 price target is grounded in a long-term DCF that assumes improving margins and sufficient cash to fund organic growth.

IDAI’s price has also changed moderately for the past six months – from $3.200 to $2.451, which is a -23.41% drop .

data.title: Buy Rating on Volatus Aerospace: Leveraging Defense Tailwinds and European Growth with a Path to Profitability

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

Matthew Galinko has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Volatus Aerospace’s growth trajectory and positioning in a strengthening unmanned systems market. Despite recent quarterly results that lagged expectations and pressured margins, the company delivered solid full-year revenue expansion, underpinned by strong equipment sales and rapid growth in European markets as a NATO-aligned supplier.

Galinko also highlights Volatus’s strategic leverage to Canada’s push to reinforce domestic defense capabilities and UAS supply chains, which he believes will translate into meaningful commercial and military demand over time. With a sizable cash position, moderating cash burn, and a path to EBITDA breakeven at higher revenue levels, he views the current valuation as attractive relative to his 2027 revenue outlook, supporting a C$1.25 price target and a Buy recommendation.

Gianluca Mozzali has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors linked to Italian Wine Brands S.p.A.’s solid fundamentals and upside potential. The group has maintained a leading position in the Italian wine industry, posting stable sales volumes with particular strength in international markets, Ho.Re.Ca. channels and Top Brands, while keeping margins resilient through an improved product mix and cost efficiencies.

data.title: Grifal: Weaker 2025 Results Offset by Gradual 2026 Recovery Prospects Justify Hold and Lowered Target Price
data.description:
Intesa Sanpaolo analyst Davide Rimini downgraded the rating on Grifal SpA to a Hold yesterday, setting a price target of €1.28.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Davide Rimini has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors that reflect both recent setbacks and medium‑term normalisation prospects. Grifal’s 2025 performance fell short of expectations, with a notable revenue contraction in the second half, margin erosion that pushed EBITDA down to low single digits, and net losses that translated into a marked increase in leverage.

At the same time, management anticipates a progressive recovery starting in 2026, driven by stricter cost management, better commercial execution, and supportive regulation favouring recyclable packaging. Rimini cuts forecasts and the target price to EUR 1.28, acknowledging the tougher macro backdrop, including weaker industrial demand, higher interest rates, and renewed input‑cost risk, which together justify a neutral stance rather than a more positive or negative view on the stock.

According to TipRanks, Rimini is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 8.7% and a 55.22% success rate. Rimini covers the Financial sector, focusing on stocks such as Generalfinance S.p.A., doValue S.p.A, and Equita Group SpA.
